page: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, and AVX-512 BF16. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, which provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

In benchmark testing, the c8a.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU evaluations, as well as Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only bandwidth for small cached blocks, while large-block uncached read and write operations fall into the top 25% category. The instance exhibits high computational efficiency for specialized tasks, achieving top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations. However, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core represents a key architectural tradeoff, limiting its suitability for memory-intensive datasets despite the high memory speed.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its high-frequency dedicated cores and advanced instruction sets, the c8a.12xlarge is designed for compute-bound workloads. It is highly effective for tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference, including medium-sized language model text generation where it maintains top-tier performance.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 96.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmark data places this server in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations. While the DDR5 memory delivers strong large-block bandwidth, the low memory allocation per core represents a clear tradeoff for memory-heavy applications. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and compilation performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier CPU benchmark performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive computational workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 cores running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier CPU, software compilation, and database performance
Provides 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Optimized for compute-bound workloads, static web serving, and CPU inference
